item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This story was interesting to say the least. The vague mentions of other SCPs that are barely discernable it amazing, and the way the story plays out is creepy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: EDIT: I can't read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually like that Pan Hun could be taken out with a gun, at least temporarily. Not EVERYTHING needs to be bulletproof and unstoppable. And it makes more sense for him to go to all the bother of pretending to be friendly before slaughtering everyone if a stray bullet could potentially take his head off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This also confused me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was an interesting read but there were two mistakes at the beginning:

"It there something bothering"

"I believe that I am your only, so where else would you be?"

Other than that I loved it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: that's all it took to subdue pan han? a pistol? I suppose I'll have to read on maybe it will reveal joshua had protection from its mental affecting abilities…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Also, did Pan Hun opened his eyes while they were still sewn?

This threw me off, too. @djkaktus I'd recommend resolving that, if you can.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The initial door has been neutralized, but more are presumed to exist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really loved the story overall and the reminder of how dangerous it is trying to reason with an SCP or try and make it serve the Foundation; however, I feel like SCP-1864-2 acts a little bit out of character.

In its SCP page, Pan Hun is described as a liar who convinced everybody of being "a messenger from God Almighty" before revealing himself. In this tale he doesn´t try to fool anybody and straight warns the Foundation about using their "toys" as they please and then attacks the rest of the characters. The only lies I could catch are his powers, which he undid after being threaten by Joshua…illusions, maybe? perhaps a sort of reality bender?

Also, did Pan Hun opened his eyes while they were still sewn?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The place Joshua threatens to send Pan Hun, since it's called "the sanctuary of sundered hearts" and has something to do with "the Door"-is this SCP-1983, "Doorway to Nowhere"? It's presumed neutralized, but in this canon that could be wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the response Moose! I suspected there would be some kind of hierarchical distinction between a pre-existing anomaly and the manufacture of anomalous humanoids that would be beyond the ken of Alpha-9.

I think it's one of those times where the complicated solution makes sense in context, but while I'm reading all the resurrection pieces I'm not quite getting that impression that something like Joshua is something they "get away with", but a commonplace feature of the Foundation. Maybe that's just me though, I don't think it's detracting from the story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kaktus should probably add a linkback to the prior story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Any ideas of speculation as to why this might be would be appreciated.

Essentially, the idea is to get across that the upper echelons of the Foundation have been flirting with the anomalous for a very, very long time. Including during the nine-year crackdown period.

Technically, most of the Factotum aren't any more anomalous than any Foundation researcher (they've almost all been affected by amnestics, remember? an anomalous technology). It's only a matter of degree.

Joshua's a more extreme example. Actually, from a certain perspective, he's worse than what's being done with Alpha-9. Alpha-9 is recruiting SCPs — SCPs which are already anomalous. But Joshua didn't start out anomalous. O5 Command made him anomalous. Because they can get away with that.

Part of the issue here is that with the proper access to materials in the mainstream Foundationverse, it is utterly trivial to become permanently anomalous, even if it's just in minor ways. And if you have access to practically infinite resources, as the O5 Council does… well, certain things become very tempting.

From that perspective, Alpha-9 is merely just one more step down that road…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In my opinion this is the best of "Alpha-Niner is Go!" so far.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought that was Pan Hun talking about Joshua.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted for the opening bit, which was just great. Tried to upvote again at the end because Joshua is the interesting kind of badass. I really love the threat he used on Pan Hun.

"This one should have told you about the dark things in the world and what happens when you pick at them like a scab."

And I adore this line.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm just seconding what was said here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the story, but I felt like it didn't accomplish much other than to show that Joshua is tough. I liked seeing 1864. I do feel like Karlyle was just sort of asking for it to join them instead of doing like heart of darkness with the cow leverage, unless there was something I missed. Overall I enjoyed it, so +1.

Also, I wouldn't say Shirley Gillespie is mine. I invented the character, but it belongs to the site for anyone to use.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Who is Joshua? I feel like I should know this.

Anyway, I like the story, so obligatory upboat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was an entertaining read as usual Kaktus, I did laugh a little at "Guten tag, Doktor Boff" but not in a way that detracted from the rest of the tale. The Pan Hun was interesting and squicky.

This tale did raise a question for me about the conceit of Resurrection though, and one I'm unsure how to solve. Joshua raises questions for me. He's blatantly anomalous, and obviously in the employ of the Foundation. Presumably he's not the only anomalous person in the Foundation, so why are we looking for ridiculously dangerous SCP objects to utilise?

I suppose I'm nitpicking with it, but it just cuts to the heart of the anomalous task force idea to have anomalous controllable humanoids guarding O5s and then force Light and Clef to search for more dangerous objects to use. Any ideas of speculation as to why this might be would be appreciated. Also, this isn't a criticism of this piece particularly, just that Joshua made me think of it.

item.title: In which an old man does crazy things
item.content:encoded:
This is Folly, my third entry into the Resurrection series, which follows Karlyle in his assignment to look for new skips what to join the O5 Council's Band of Merry Men. Co-starring the enigmatic Joshua, and Roget's Director Gillespie.

Fun for all, no doubt! Many thanks to thedeadlymoose, Decibelle, Clef, Light, Photosynthetic, KPC, and any others who reviewed this in chat. I very much appreciate it.
